title: Weltgewebe edge projection performance evidence
goal: Measure the remaining backend and map projection costs after PR #1461 removes repeated timestamp parsing, and introduce indexes or MapLibre feature-state updates only when reproducible target-scale evidence proves their benefit.
completion: A reproducible 500,000-edge backend profile covers unfiltered lists, sparse endpoint filters, cursor pagination and account relation projections with CPU, latency and memory evidence.
Any secondary source, target or expiry index is justified by an exceeded budget and remains consistent across JSONL and PostgreSQL create, refresh, replay and removal paths.
Browser profiles cover 250, 1,000 and 10,000 representative map edges on target devices before replacing periodic GeoJSON updates with feature-state.
Exact expires_at removal, filter correctness and style-reload behavior remain proven under any accepted optimization.

metadata.source: Critical review and measured follow-up analysis of Weltgewebe PR #1461 on 2026-07-17
metadata.safety_boundary: Registration does not authorize a cache index, browser rendering rewrite, API limit increase, merge or deployment. Optimization requires reproducible evidence and must preserve lifecycle truth.
does_not_establish: that the current linear scans violate a production budget
that setData currently causes visible jank at the 250-edge map limit
permission to increase browser data limits
